frame_analyses.description: VirtaMed simulator title screen showing 'Uterine Vessel Coagulation'
Laparoscopic view of uterine anatomy with electrocautery device approaching tissue
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper instrument positioning for vessel coagulation

frame_analyses.description: Active coagulation of left uterine vessels with visible thermal effect
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appropriate coagulation technique with controlled energy application

frame_analyses.description: Sequential coagulation and cutting of uterine vessels
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Systematic approach to vessel division after adequate coagulation

confidence_rationale: Clear identification of VirtaMed simulation interface and uterine vessel coagulation procedure. This is definitively not an FLS task but rather a gynecologic laparoscopy training module.
technique_summary: This video demonstrates a VirtaMed simulation of uterine vessel coagulation, which is not part of the standard FLS task battery.

demonstrated_techniques: proper electrocautery positioning for vessel coagulation
verbal_cues: Make sure to expose and mobilize the uterine artery and vein at the ascending portion using blunt and sharp dissection until it can be safely identified
Sufficiently coagulate and cut the left uterine vessels close to the uterus (about 1cm). Make sure not to cause any bleeding
common_errors_addressed: avoiding thermal injury to adjacent structures
